Table.FromRecords.signature: Table.FromRecords(records as list, optional columns as any, optional missingField as MissingField.Type) -> table
Table.FromRecords.description: Converts a list of record values into a table where each record becomes a row and record fields become columns. Handles missing fields according to the specified MissingField strategy.

Table.SelectRows.signature: Table.SelectRows(table as table, condition as function) -> table
Table.SelectRows.description: Returns a table containing only the rows from the input table where the condition function evaluates to true, providing predicate-based row filtering for data transformation pipelines.

Table.AddColumn.signature: Table.AddColumn(table as table, newColumnName as text, columnGenerator as function, optional columnType as type) -> table
Table.AddColumn.description: Adds a new column to the table with values computed by applying the generator function to each row, supporting derived columns and calculated fields in transformation steps.

Table.TransformColumns.signature: Table.TransformColumns(table as table, transformOperations as list, optional defaultTransformation, optional missingField) -> table
Table.TransformColumns.description: Applies transformation functions to the specified columns of a table, enabling type conversions, formatting changes, and value modifications across entire columns of data.

Table.Group.signature: Table.Group(table as table, key as any, aggregatedColumns as list, optional groupKind, optional comparer) -> table
Table.Group.description: Groups rows by the specified key columns and applies aggregate operations such as sum, count, and list to the remaining columns, equivalent to SQL GROUP BY functionality.

Table.Join.signature: Table.Join(table1 as table, key1 as any, table2 as table, key2 as any, optional joinKind as JoinKind.Type, optional joinAlgorithm, optional keyEqualityComparers) -> table
Table.Join.description: Combines rows from two tables based on matching key values, supporting inner, left outer, right outer, full outer, left anti, and right anti join operations.

List.Transform.signature: List.Transform(list as list, transform as function) -> list
List.Transform.description: Applies a transformation function to each element of the input list and returns a new list containing the transformed values, enabling functional mapping operations on collections.

List.Select.signature: List.Select(list as list, selection as function) -> list
List.Select.description: Returns a new list containing only the elements from the input list where the selection function returns true, providing predicate-based filtering for list values.

List.Accumulate.signature: List.Accumulate(list as list, seed as any, accumulator as function) -> any
List.Accumulate.description: Reduces a list to a single value by applying an accumulator function that takes the current state and each element, producing a running aggregation similar to fold or reduce.

Record.Field.signature: Record.Field(record as record, field as text) -> any
Record.Field.description: Returns the value of the specified field from the given record. Raises an error if the field does not exist, unlike the optional Record.FieldOrDefault which handles missing fields.

Record.TransformFields.signature: Record.TransformFields(record as record, transformOperations as list, optional missingField) -> record
Record.TransformFields.description: Returns a new record with the specified fields transformed by applying the provided functions, enabling selective modification of record field values during data cleaning.

Text.Contains.signature: Text.Contains(text as nullable text, substring as text, optional comparer as function) -> logical
Text.Contains.description: Returns true if the text value contains the specified substring. Supports an optional comparer function for case-insensitive or culture-aware string matching operations.

Text.Combine.signature: Text.Combine(texts as list, optional separator as nullable text) -> text
Text.Combine.description: Concatenates a list of text values into a single string with an optional separator between each element, useful for building delimited strings from list-based data.

Number.From.signature: Number.From(value as any, optional culture as nullable text) -> nullable number
Number.From.description: Converts a text, logical, date, time, or other value to a number using the specified culture for locale-aware parsing of decimal separators and number formatting conventions.

Type.Is.signature: Type.Is(type1 as type, type2 as type) -> logical
Type.Is.description: Returns true if type1 is compatible with type2, used for runtime type checking and conditional logic based on the data types of values flowing through query transformations.

each.description: Shorthand syntax in Power Query M for creating a single-parameter function where the underscore represents the parameter. Equivalent to writing (_) => expression for concise lambda definitions.

String.description: Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

Number.description: Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

Boolean.description: Logical type with two values: true and false.

Array.description: Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

Object.description: Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

null.description: Represents the intentional absence of any value.
